key_insight: Conforming finite-element matrices can be treated as black-box SPD systems for LS-AMG-DD because elementwise SPSD energy splittings imply local Gram representations, enabling multilevel algebraic preconditioning.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical preconditioner for difficult solid-mechanics finite-element problems, including high-order and anisotropic systems where classical AMG can stall or require extremely many iterations.
method: Identify a cover of degrees of freedom and build a local SPSD splitting of the stiffness matrix.
Use local spectral problems to construct interpolation and form coarse operators via the induced Gram factorization.
Apply the framework to conforming FEM by extracting element-local algebraic factors from assembled element blocks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports robust performance on grad-div in H(div), anisotropic hyperdiffusion in H^2, and linear elasticity in vector H^1, with classical AMG sometimes exceeding 10^5 iterations and LS-AMG-DD often yielding 2–5 orders of magnitude smaller errors at the same residual tolerance.
watch_item: The method depends on obtaining suitable local Gram/SPSD factorizations on the chosen cover, so implementation quality and the choice of element-level factors may affect robustness and setup cost.
paper_title: A black-box, multilevel algebraic preconditioning framework for conforming finite elements
